body:
Evidence Handling Procedure

General procedure for receiving, labeling, and storing physical evidence at the McKinley County Sheriff's Office.

  • Log each item on intake with a unique evidence number.
  • Store in the secured evidence room; restrict access to authorized personnel.
  • Record every transfer in the property ledger.
title: Evidence Handling Procedure

body:
Chain of Custody Procedure

General standard for maintaining an unbroken chain of custody for any item entered into evidence. Applies to all investigations.

  • Document each person who takes possession, with date and time of transfer.
  • Require a signature for every hand-off.
  • Note the reason for each movement (analysis, court, return).
title: Chain of Custody Procedure

body:
Report Filing Standards

General standards for drafting and filing incident and investigative reports.

  • File the initial report within the shift it occurred.
  • Use plain factual language; separate observation from conclusion.
  • Route to the supervising officer for review before closing.
title: Report Filing Standards

body:
Equipment Request Workflow

General workflow for requesting and tracking field and office equipment.

  • Submit the request form to the operations desk.
  • Operations confirms availability and assigns the item.
  • Return and check-in is logged at end of assignment.
title: Equipment Request Workflow

body:
Records Request Workflow

General workflow for handling internal and external records requests.

  • Verify the requester's authority and the legal basis.
  • Locate responsive records through the records clerk.
  • Apply redactions per policy before release.
title: Records Request Workflow

body:
Interview Room Booking Procedure

General procedure for reserving and using interview rooms.

  • Reserve the room through the shared calendar.
  • Confirm recording equipment is functional before the session.
  • Release the room and log usage when finished.
title: Interview Room Booking Procedure

body:
Investigative Case Opening Procedure

General procedure for opening a new investigation file.

  • Assign a case number and responsible investigator.
  • Record the initial complaint and jurisdiction.
  • Set up the evidence and document index.
title: Investigative Case Opening Procedure

body:
Witness Interview Procedure Standards

General standards for conducting witness interviews.

  • Advise the witness of the purpose of the interview.
  • Record the interview where permitted.
  • Summarize and have the witness review the statement.
title: Witness Interview Procedure Standards

body:
Interview Procedure Standards

General standards governing suspect and subject interviews across cooperating agencies.

  • Confirm advisement of rights is documented.
  • Use neutral, non-leading questioning.
  • Preserve all recordings as evidence per the retention policy.
title: Interview Procedure Standards

body:
Evidence Log Review Procedure

General procedure for periodic review of evidence logs to confirm completeness.

  • Reconcile the property ledger against stored items.
  • Flag any gaps in the chain-of-custody entries for follow-up.
  • Document the review and sign off.
title: Evidence Log Review Procedure

body:
Missing Persons Intake Procedure

General intake procedure for missing-persons reports.

  • Complete the standard intake form with reporter and subject details.
  • Enter the report into the applicable databases without delay.
  • Assign an investigator and set initial follow-up steps.
title: Missing Persons Intake Procedure

body:
Records Retention Procedure

General procedure describing how long case records and evidence are retained.

  • Apply the retention schedule by record category.
  • Review files for eligible destruction annually.
  • Document disposition with supervisor approval.
title: Records Retention Procedure

body:
Interagency Coordination Protocol

General protocol for coordinating an investigation across multiple agencies.

  • Designate a lead agency and a single point of contact.
  • Agree on information-sharing channels and formats.
  • Document joint actions in a shared coordination log.
title: Interagency Coordination Protocol

body:
Mutual Aid Request Procedure

General procedure for requesting and providing mutual aid between agencies.

  • Submit the request through the designated liaison.
  • Confirm scope, duration, and resources in writing.
  • Debrief and document after the aid period.
title: Mutual Aid Request Procedure

body:
Patrol Shift Coverage Procedure

General procedure for planning patrol shift coverage.

  • Build the rotation against minimum staffing levels.
  • Record swaps and approvals in the schedule.
  • Confirm coverage for holidays and special events in advance.
title: Patrol Shift Coverage Procedure

body:
Shift Handover Procedure

General procedure for handing over an active shift.

  • Brief the incoming officer on open matters.
  • Account for assigned equipment and vehicles.
  • Note outstanding follow-ups in the handover log.
title: Shift Handover Procedure

body:
Procedure Review Notes (draft)

Personal working notes for tidying up the cross-agency procedure pages before sharing.

  • Cross-check that every procedure has an owner.
  • Confirm interview and evidence pages link to the retention policy.
  • Ask Thomas Kee and Rita Benally to review.
title: Draft — Procedure Review Notes

body:
Evidence Storage Policy

General policy governing the physical storage and security of evidence.

  • Maintain restricted, logged access to storage areas.
  • Segregate biological, hazardous, and high-value items per guidance.
  • Audit storage on a fixed schedule.
title: Evidence Storage Policy

body:
Records Disposal Policy

General policy on the secure disposal of records past their retention period.

  • Verify eligibility against the retention schedule.
  • Use approved secure destruction methods.
  • Record the disposal with witness and approval.
title: Records Disposal Policy

body:
Digital Evidence Handling Policy

General policy for acquiring and preserving digital evidence.

  • Create and verify forensic copies before analysis.
  • Maintain hash values to demonstrate integrity.
  • Store originals in read-only secured media.
title: Digital Evidence Handling Policy
